activeIngredients.name: Lactobacillus gasseri
activeIngredients.notes: A probiotic strain that may help with weight management and metabolic health in addition to digestive benefits.

activeIngredients.name: Bifidobacterium longum
activeIngredients.notes: A key probiotic strain that colonizes the colon, supporting immune and digestive health. Often included in comprehensive probiotic formulations.

activeIngredients.name: Bifidobacterium bifidum
activeIngredients.notes: A beneficial probiotic strain naturally present in the human gut microbiome, supporting digestive health and immune function. Known for its ability to help maintain healthy gut barrier function.

inactive_ingredients.name: Hypromellose (derived from Cellulose) Capsule
inactive_ingredients.notes: Generic capsule delivery format descriptor. Not a specific ingredient but a formulation type. Classified as non-active to prevent unmapped inflation.

inactive_ingredients.name: Potato Starch
inactive_ingredients.notes: Natural starch from potatoes, used as a binder and thickener. Gluten-free and generally hypoallergenic.

inactive_ingredients.name: Silica
inactive_ingredients.notes: Amorphous silicon dioxide is the most common anti-caking agent in supplement manufacturing. FDA GRAS at ≤2%. EFSA 2018 data gap request was precautionary — no finding of harm. Low severity — tracked purely as non-nutritive excipient quality signal.
inactive_ingredients.mechanism_of_harm: Amorphous silicon dioxide (SiO2) used as anti-caking agent in tablets/capsules at <2% w/w. FDA GRAS (21 CFR 172.480, max 2% of food weight). EFSA 2018 re-evaluation could not confirm safety due to insufficient nanoparticle characterization data — this was a data gap, not a finding of harm. Amorphous SiO2 is poorly absorbed from the GI tract. Nanoparticle concerns are based on in vitro studies at concentrations far exceeding dietary exposure.
population_warnings: No specific population concerns at anti-caking agent levels (<2% w/w)
inactive_ingredients.harmful_notes: Amorphous silicon dioxide (SiO2) used as anti-caking agent in tablets/capsules at <2% w/w. FDA GRAS (21 CFR 172.480, max 2% of food weight). EFSA 2018 re-evaluation could not confirm safety due to insufficient nanoparticle characterization data — this was a data gap, not a finding of harm. Amorphous SiO2 is poorly absorbed from the GI tract. Nanoparticle concerns are based on in vitro studies at concentrations far exceeding dietary exposure.

warnings.title: Contains Silicon Dioxide (E551)
warnings.detail: Amorphous silicon dioxide (SiO2) used as anti-caking agent in tablets/capsules at <2% w/w. FDA GRAS (21 CFR 172.480, max 2% of food weight). EFSA 2018 re-evaluation could not confirm safety due to insufficient nanoparticle characterization data — this was a data gap, not a finding of harm. Amorphous SiO2 is poorly absorbed from the GI tract. Nanoparticle concerns are based on in vitro studies at concentrations far exceeding dietary exposure.
warnings.notes: Amorphous silicon dioxide is the most common anti-caking agent in supplement manufacturing. FDA GRAS at ≤2%. EFSA 2018 data gap request was precautionary — no finding of harm. Low severity — tracked purely as non-nutritive excipient quality signal.

EFSA 2018 re-evaluation could not confirm safety due to insufficient nanoparticle characterization data — this was a data gap, not a finding of harm. Amorphous SiO2 is poorly absorbed from the GI tract. Nanoparticle concerns are based on in vitro studies at concentrations far exceeding dietary exposure.
population_warnings: No specific population concerns at anti-caking agent levels (<2% w/w)

key_endpoints: Reduced IBS-SSS score by 43.4% after 8 weeks with >60% achieving clinically meaningful reductions (>50-point decrease) using B. longum 35624 strain (PMID: 37702965)
Decreased IBS severity (IBS-SSS 208 vs 303 baseline, p<0.001) with 57% achieving lower severity categories or remission at 30 days (PMID: 35317278)
Attenuated cortisol output and subjective anxiety in response to social stress after 4 weeks at 1x10^9 CFU/day of B. longum 1714 in healthy volunteers (PMID: 27801892)
Improved sleep quality (PSQI) and reduced daytime dysfunction at 4 weeks, with further energy/vitality improvements at 8 weeks vs placebo using B. longum 1714 (PMID: 38341968)
clinical_matches.effect_direction_rationale: Retained positive_strong classification; study_type=rct_single; evidence_level=ingredient-human; primary_outcome=Digestive Health; published_tags=RCT; published_studies_count=131; registry_completed_trials_count=131; total_enrollment=583; endpoint_tags=immune_support,stress_mood,glycemic_control,digestive_health; notes/citations contain no automated downgrade signals.
clinical_matches.notes: B. longum has strong strain-specific evidence: 35624 strain for IBS symptom relief (PMID: 37702965, 35317278), and 1714 strain for gut-brain axis effects including stress response and sleep quality (PMID: 27801892, 38341968). Strain specificity is critical for clinical translation.
clinical_matches.notable_studies: NCT04706559 (n=98): Efficacy of Oral Supplementation of Probiotics in Children With Atopic Dermatitis. NCT05367427 (n=60): Heat-treated Postbiotic Consumption in Healthy People With Mild to Moderate Gastrointestinal Symptoms. NCT04422327 (n=40): The Impact of a Combination of Bifidobacterium Longum 35624® and 1714™ Strains in Adults With Irritable Bowel Syndrome.

references_structured.title: Efficacy of Oral Supplementation of Probiotics in Children With Atopic Dermatitis
Heat-treated Postbiotic Consumption in Healthy People With Mild to Moderate Gastrointestinal Symptoms
The Impact of a Combination of Bifidobacterium Longum 35624® and 1714™ Strains in Adults With Irritable Bowel Syndrome

key_endpoints: Reduced global IBS symptoms by 0.88 points vs 0.16 placebo (p<0.0001) with 57% responder rate vs 21% placebo in 4-week RCT of MIMBb75 strain (n=122) (PMID: 21418261)
34% achieved composite endpoint (>=30% abdominal pain improvement + adequate relief in >=4/8 weeks) vs 19% placebo (RR 1.7, p=0.0007) in 443-patient multicenter RCT (PMID: 32277872)
Improved mental flexibility scores (p<0.05) and increased serum BDNF levels (p<0.05) at 12 weeks in 63 older adults receiving BGN4 strain (PMID: 32300799)
Decreased SCORAD index and increased gut Bacteroidetes proportion in 109 atopic dermatitis patients with CCFM16 strain (PMID: 31342226)
clinical_matches.effect_direction_rationale: Retained positive_strong classification; study_type=rct_multiple; evidence_level=ingredient-human; primary_outcome=Digestive Health; published_tags=RCT; published_studies_count=73; registry_completed_trials_count=73; total_enrollment=583; endpoint_tags=immune_support,sleep,digestive_health; notes/citations contain no automated downgrade signals.
clinical_matches.notes: B. bifidum has strong IBS evidence from the MIMBb75 strain: landmark 122-patient RCT (PMID: 21418261) and confirmatory 443-patient multicenter trial (PMID: 32277872). Also shows cognitive/BDNF benefits (BGN4 strain, PMID: 32300799) and skin-related immune modulation. Strain specificity matters.
clinical_matches.notable_studies: NCT01709825 (n=583): The Effect of Probiotics (L. Helveticus, B. Longum, and B. Bifidum) on Immune Function and Digestive Health. NCT06035835 (n=437): Probiotics and Breastmilk Decrease Risk of Atopic Dermatitis in Premature Infants. NCT04958460 (n=150): Effect of Probiotics on ADHD.

references_structured.title: The Effect of Probiotics (L. Helveticus, B. Longum, and B. Bifidum) on Immune Function and Digestive Health
Probiotics and Breastmilk Decrease Risk of Atopic Dermatitis in Premature Infants

clusters.note: +1 synergy bonus applies when at least 2 ingredients from 'Probiotic and Gut Health' are matched and at least half of dose-checkable ingredients meet minimum effective doses. Evidence strength: Tier 1 (strong). Anchor dose checks: probiotics >= 1e+09, saccharomyces boulardii >= 5e+09, inulin >= 5000. See sources for supporting references.
